orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.content:
**Orcas Island Forecast – Friday, Feb 20**

☁️ Mostly cloudy, high 41°F, low 37°F. Wind chill 25-35°F.

**Ferry Travel:** Excellent conditions. Light SE winds (6 mph) make for smooth crossings. Evening winds pick up slightly (7-10 mph E) but remain calm. No delays expected.

**Outdoor Activities:** Dress warmly—wind chill is brisk. Dry conditions until after 4am when light rain/snow showers possible (30% chance, minimal accumulation).

**Heads Up:** Saturday brings stronger winds with 22 mph gusts and increased rain/snow chances. Plan ferry trips and outdoor activities for Friday if possible.

| Condition | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| **High** | 41°F |
| **Low** | 37°F (overnight) |
| **Sky** | Mostly cloudy |
| **Precipitation** | 30% chance of rain/snow showers after 4am Friday night; little to no snow accumulation |
| **Wind (Day)** | Southeast ~6 mph (light) |
| **Wind (Night)** | East 7-10 mph |
| **Wind Chill** | 25-35°F |

### Island Travel Conditions
**Ferry-friendly day** – Light winds (6 mph SE) during the day make for calm conditions on the ferries. Evening brings slightly stronger east winds (7-10 mph) but still manageable. No major wind gusts expected until Saturday (22 mph gusts).

### Looking Ahead
Saturday brings increasing precipitation (40% chance rain/snow mix) with stronger winds and gusts up to 22 mph. Sunday onward shows rain likely through early next week.

Open, mostly cloudy, 37°F. Rain/snow mix possible Friday night.

**I-5 Seattle Corridor** ✅ CLEAR
Normal conditions. Slight chance overnight snow/rain. May be slick.

---

**Ferry Service**

Small craft advisory ended. No disruptions.

**Seattle/Bremerton** ⚠️ DELAYS
Feb 20 10:15pm sailing canceled.

Construction delays through July 2026.

wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
Based on all the information gathered, here's the **Washington State Road Conditions Report** for **February 19, 2026**:

---

## 🚗 Washington State Road Conditions Summary

### **I-90 — Snoqualmie Pass**
- **Weather Conditions:** Snow likely today (60% chance), mainly between 7am-4pm. High near 27°F, low around 12°F tonight
- **Accumulation:** Less than half inch expected today; heavier snow (1+ inches) expected Saturday-Sunday
- **Advisory:** Carry chains. Winter conditions expected through the week with significant snow Sunday-Monday

### **Highway 20 — North Cascades**
- ⚠️ **SEASONAL CLOSURE:** Highway 20 (North Cascades Highway) is **typically closed** from mid-November through late April/early May due to heavy winter snowpack
- **Weather (area):** Snow likely (60% today), with temperatures as low as -8°F wind chill. Heavy snow expected Monday
- **Status:** Assume closed; verify reopening in late spring

### **SR-20 to Anacortes (San Juan Islands Ferry Terminal)**
- **Status:** OPEN - No closures reported
- **Weather:** Mostly cloudy, high 37°F, wind chill 20-29°F. North winds 11 mph with gusts to 21 mph
- **Weekend:** Rain/snow mix possible Friday night, transitioning to rain Saturday-Sunday

### **I-5 — Seattle Corridor**
- **Status:** OPEN - Normal conditions
- **Weather:** Slight chance of rain/snow showers overnight and Thursday morning. High 39°F, low 29°F
- **Advisory:** Roads may be slick overnight. Expect rain throughout the weekend

---

## ⛴️ Ferry Service (Anacortes/San Juan Islands)
- **Small Craft Advisory** in effect until 10am this morning for marine areas
- **Wave conditions:** Around 1 ft, improving
- **No major service disruptions** reported for Anacortes terminal

### Other Ferry Alerts:
- **Seattle/Bremerton:** Feb 20 — 10:15pm Bremerton→Seattle **CANCELED** (refueling)
- **Kingston Terminal:** Construction through July 2026; 20-30 min delays on Tue/Wed/Thu 9:40pm sailing
- **SR-104 near Kingston:** Weekday construction delays possible

---

## ⚠️ Key Recommendations:
1. **North Cascades (Hwy 20):** Plan alternate route — road is seasonally closed
2. **Snoqualmie Pass (I-90):** Check conditions before travel, carry chains, expect delays
3. **Ferry travel:** Book ahead; check wsdot.com/ferries for real-time vessel positions
4. **Monitor conditions:** Visit [wsdot.com/travel/real-time](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time) or call 511

travel-summary.content:
# Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

## Overall Recommendation: GOOD TRAVEL DAY ✅

Conditions are favorable for Friday travel. Depart in the morning for the smoothest experience.

---

**Current Conditions:** 36°F, partly cloudy, 92% humidity, light winds. Cold but clear—solid baseline for tomorrow.

**Route Status:** All segments clear.
- I-5 Seattle Corridor: Normal traffic, minor overnight slick possible
- SR-20 to Anacortes: Open, 37°F expected
- Anacortes Ferry: No disruptions, calm seas
- Orcas Island: 41°F high, dry until after 4am Saturday

**Optimal Departure:** 8:00–10:00 AM
Morning offers calmest conditions with light winds and dry roads. Arrives Anacortes with buffer for midday ferry. Avoid evening sailings after 8pm when conditions may deteriorate.

**Weather Concerns:**
- 30% chance of rain/snow mix Friday evening
- Roads may be slick overnight
- Saturday brings 22 mph wind gusts and increased precipitation—plan return for Friday or early Saturday if possible

**Pack:** Warm layers, wind-resistant jacket, waterproof outer layer, full gas tank, emergency kit. Book ferry reservations in advance at wsf.wa.gov.

**Closures (not on your route):** Highway 20 North Cascades closed; I-90 Snoqualmie Pass requires chains.

---

**Bottom Line:** Go Friday morning. The real concern is Saturday's weather deterioration—plan your return accordingly or expect potential ferry delays.
travel-summary.rawContent:
# 🚗⛴️ Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

## 1️⃣ Current Conditions (Seattle)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Temperature | 36°F (feels like 29°F) |
| Sky | Partly Cloudy |
| Humidity | 92% |
| Wind | 10 mph N |
| Visibility | 9 mi |

**Status:** Cold but clear. Good baseline for tomorrow.

---

## 2️⃣ Tomorrow's Travel Outlook

### Route: Seattle → SR-20 → Anacortes Ferry → Orcas Island

| Segment | Status | Conditions |
|---------|--------|------------|
| **I-5 Seattle Corridor** | ✅ CLEAR | Normal, slight overnight slick possible |
| **SR-20 to Anacortes** | ✅ CLEAR | 37°F, rain/snow mix possible Friday night |
| **Anacortes-San Juan Ferry** | ✅ CLEAR | No disruptions, calm seas |
| **Orcas Island** | ✅ GOOD | 41°F high, dry until after 4am Saturday |

⚠️ **Avoid:** Highway 20 North Cascades (CLOSED) and I-90 Snoqualmie Pass (chains required, not on your route)

---

## 3️⃣ Recommended Departure Time

**🎯 Optimal: 8:00 - 10:00 AM**

- Morning has calmest conditions (light winds, dry)
- Arrives Anacortes with buffer for midday ferry
- Avoids evening rain/snow mix (30% chance after 10pm)
- Ferry conditions excellent all day; evening winds pick up slightly but remain manageable

**⚠️ Avoid:** Evening sailings after 8pm when conditions may deteriorate

---

## 4️⃣ What to Pack/Prepare

| Category | Items |
|----------|-------|
| **Clothing** | Warm layers, wind-resistant jacket (wind chill 25-35°F on island) |
| **Weather Gear** | Umbrella, waterproof outer layer |
| **Vehicle** | Full gas tank, emergency kit |
| **Ferry** | Book reservations in advance at wsf.wa.gov |
| **Optional** | Hand warmers, thermos with hot beverage |

---

## 5️⃣ Alerts & Concerns

| Priority | Alert |
|----------|-------|
| 🟡 **WATCH** | Rain/snow mix possible Friday evening (30% chance) |
| 🟡 **WATCH** | Roads may be slick overnight—morning departure preferred |
| 🔴 **PLAN AHEAD** | Saturday brings 22 mph wind gusts + increased rain/snow—**return Friday or early Saturday if possible** |
| ℹ️ **INFO** | Seattle/Bremerton 10:15pm sailing canceled (not on your route) |

---

## ✅ Bottom Line

**GO — Conditions are favorable.** Travel Friday morning for the smoothest experience. The real concern is **Saturday's weather deterioration**—plan your return trip accordingly or be prepared for potential ferry delays.
